metadata.degree_status_required_phrase: Ph.D. candidate, Physics, University of Missouri, expected May 2026
metadata.evidence_policy: Use only claims supported by this file, source CV sections, teaching philosophy materials, user-provided updates, or job postings for employer needs.
metadata.privacy_policy: No private phone number or address in default exports. Professional contacts are omitted by default.

identity.name: David Beckwitt
identity.current_positioning: Physics Ph.D. candidate specializing in quantitative X-ray scattering, thin-film materials characterization, instrument operation, Python-based modeling, and machine-learning-assisted structure analysis.
identity.short_positioning: Quantitative physicist with experience in X-ray scattering instrumentation, Python data analysis, materials characterization, and technical communication.

education.dissertation: Investigating Disorder in van der Waals Thin Films

experience.title: Graduate Research Assistant
experience.focus: X-ray diffraction instrumentation, GIWAXS modeling, thin-film growth, and machine-learning-based structure analysis.
supported_claims: Operates and maintains custom-built in-house X-ray diffraction and reflectivity systems for materials characterization.
Builds Python-based GIWAXS modeling frameworks.
Extracts site occupancies, anisotropic Debye-Waller factors, mosaicity, and geometric parameters from scattering data.
Models diffuse scattering from stacking faults and quantifies defect densities.
Grew phase-controlled PbI2 films by chemical vapor deposition and validated polytype fractions.
Develops convolutional neural networks on simulated GIWAXS data using PyTorch for automated structure analysis.

experience.title: Research Intern
supported_claims: Synthesized graphene films by PLD or PVD.
Characterized films with Raman spectroscopy and electron microscopy.

experience.title: Research Assistant
supported_claims: Designed and built a pulsed laser deposition system.
Characterized thin films by XRD, Raman spectroscopy, SEM/EDS, and profilometry.

experience.title: R&D Intern
supported_claims: Developed software for biomedical testing.
Automated hardware systems.

projects.name: 2D_Mosaic_Sim
supported_claims: Visualizes diffraction patterns for materials with specific crystal orientations.
Supports analysis beyond standard software through custom plotting and interpretation tools.

projects.name: ra_sim
supported_claims: Simulates and analyzes diffraction data from R-Axis IV++ detectors.
Combines a focused interface with optimization methods tailored to this detector format.

projects.name: OSC_Reader
supported_claims: Converts proprietary detector files into accessible formats.
Enables detailed inspection of diffraction images without commercial software.

instrumentation_and_materials: neutron scattering as listed skill only

publications.citation: Christopher J. Arendse, Randy Burns, David Beckwitt, Dallar Babaian, Ping Yu, Suchismita Guha, Daniel Hill, Zahid Hossain, and Paul Miceli, Insights into the Growth Orientation and Phase Stability of Chemical-Vapor-Deposited Two-Dimensional Hybrid Halide Perovskite Films, ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces 15(50), 56692-56703, 2023.
David Beckwitt et al., Quantitative Simulation and Refinement of Diffraction from 2D Oriented Powders, anticipated Summer 2026.

presentations: Quantitative X-ray Diffraction Analysis of 2D-Oriented Powders with Area Detectors, 2025.
Invited talks and presentations on X-ray diffraction at APS Prairie Section 2023, APS March Meeting 2024, ACNS 2024, and Missouri State University Seminar 2024.
Fabrication and Characterization of 2D Heterostructures of Graphene and Transition-Metal Oxides, 2020.
Undergraduate poster presentations on thin-film materials, 2017-2018.

roles.title: Academic Tutor
supported_claims: Individual and small-group tutoring in undergraduate physics.

roles.title: Teaching Assistant
supported_claims: Taught and assisted physics courses with enrollments from 15 to 200+ students.
Selected courses include introductory algebra- and calculus-based mechanics, electricity and magnetism, and classical mechanics.

roles.title: ACT Prep Tutor
supported_claims: Individualized test-preparation instruction.

roles.title: Martial Arts Coach
supported_claims: Led skills instruction and student development across ages and experience levels.

methods_from_teaching_philosophy: lesson planning around objectives, assessment, activities, and feedback

service_and_outreach: Sigma Pi Sigma Physics Congress Judge.
Public STEM engagement through MU Extension eclipse events, STEM Cubs, Moberly Correctional Center, and Columbia Young Scientist Expo.
Missouri State University CNAS Leadership Board and Sigma Pi Sigma President.
SPS high school engagement, physics demonstrations, and student mentoring.
awards: Research and Creative Activity Forum Award, 2025.
Ron Boain and Catherine Rangel-Boain Travel Award, 2023.
claims_requiring_confirmation_before_use: Customer revenue ownership or sales quotas.
K-12 teaching license, certification, endorsement, AP Physics, IB, or dual-credit teaching.
Student evaluation scores, pass rates, retention effects, or measured learning gains without data.
Cloud deployment, MLOps ownership, or production-scale software engineering.
Managed employees or led a commercial team.
